ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Maximus, Bishop of Salona.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Maximus, &c.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: When our common son the presbyter Veteranus came to the Roman city, he found me so weak from the pains of gout as to be quite unable to answer thy Fraternity’s letters myself.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And indeed with regard to the nation of the Sclaves, from which you are in great danger, I am exceedingly afflicted and disturbed.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I am afflicted as suffering already in your suffering: I am disturbed, because they have already begun to enter Italy by way of Istria.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Further, of Julian the scribo, what shall I say, seeing that I see everywhere how our sins find us out, so as to cause us to be disturbed by the nations from without and by judges from within?

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But be not at all saddened by such things, since those who shall live after us will see worse times; so much so, that they will regard us as having had happy days in comparison with their own.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, so far as thy Fraternity has power, thou oughtest to oppose thyself in behalf of the poor, in behalf of the oppressed.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, even if thou shouldest be unable to do any good, the very devotion of thy heart, which Almighty God has given, is enough for Him.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For it is written, Rescue them that are drawn unto death, and forbear not to deliver them that are ready to be slain (Prov. xxiv.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But if thou shouldest say, My powers are insufficient, He who sees into the heart understands.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: In all that thou doest, then, desire to have Him Who sees into the heart well-pleased with thee.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But whatever there is whereby He may be pleased omit not thou to do.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For human terrors and favours are like smoke, which is snatched by a light breeze and vanishes away.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Know this most assuredly, that no one can please God and bad men.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Let, therefore, thy Fraternity esteem thyself to have pleased Almighty God in such degree as thou knowest thyself to have displeased froward men.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Yet let thy defence of the poor itself be moderate and grave, lest, if anything be done too rigidly, men should think you actuated by the pride of youth.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But our defence of the poor must needs be found of such sort that both the humble may feel protection and oppressors may not easily find what out of a malevolent disposition they may blame.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Attend, then, to what is said to Ezekiel, Son of man, unbelievers and destroyers are with thee, and thou dost dwell among scorpions (Ezek. ii.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And the blessed Job says, I have been a brother of dragons, and a companion of owls (Job xxx.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And Paul says to his disciples, In the midst of a crooked and perverse nation, among whom ye shine as lights in the world (Philip. ii.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: We ought, then, to walk all the more cautiously as we know that we are living among the enemies of God.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Further, with regard to the Photinianists, let thy Fraternity pay the utmost attention; and, as thou hast begun, study how to recall them to the bosom of holy Church.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, if any should wish to come to me, and to receive an explanation, let them first make oath that they will not permit their followers to persist in their error even after an explanation has been received.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And then let thy Holiness promise them that they will suffer no wrong from me, but that I will give them an explanation.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: If they should acknowledge the truth, let them accept it; if they should not acknowledge it, I will dismiss them unharmed.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, if any of them should wish to come to us against you, let thy Fraternity by no means detain them; for, when they come, they shall either accept an explanation, or assuredly they will not see that land any more.
